description_long: This object consists of a group of round, flat plates of varying diameters, arranged in a grid and displayed on a flat surface with measurement scales along two edges. The plates feature a range of earthy tones, including terracotta, brown, and ochre, with some displaying intricate painted or printed designs, possibly floral or abstract motifs. The surfaces of the plates show visible signs of wear, such as scratches, faded areas, and some chipped edges, suggesting they have been used or aged. The plates are made of ceramic or earthenware, with a matte to slightly glossy finish, and their thickness appears consistent with traditional dinnerware. The measurement scales indicate that the largest plates are approximately 0.3 meters in diameter, while the smallest are closer to 0.08 meters. The overall presentation suggests these plates could be found in an urban environment, such as a street market, outdoor café, or as discarded items on a sidewalk.
description: A collection of worn, painted ceramic plates in various sizes and earthy tones, arranged on a flat surface with measurement scales. The plates display decorative motifs and visible signs of use.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the plates from a low, front-facing angle, highlighting the decorative surfaces and the full range of plate sizes.
description_view_1: View 1 presents the plates from a left-side angle, emphasizing the depth and arrangement of the plates in rows.
description_view_2: View 2 displays the plates from the back, showing more of the plain, unadorned surfaces and the measurement scale along the top.
description_view_3: View 3 offers a right-side perspective, revealing the plate edges and the distribution of decorative patterns across the group.
attribute_visibility_of_markings_or_insc: Decorative patterns and painted motifs are visible on the upper surfaces of several plates.
attribute_surrounding_context: Plates are arranged on a flat surface with measurement scales along two edges.
